compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can rely on intent-search to validate bug behavioral claims against actual callers and API contracts
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Enhance the existing dso:intent-search agent to add a caller/contract validation pass after the historical search. When intent-search classifies a bug as intent-aligned historically, it then traces callers of affected functions, checks type signatures and usage patterns, and validates whether the bug's "should" claims conflict with actual caller behavior.
**Why**: Bugs authored by agents sometimes contain incorrect "should" assumptions. The current intent-search only checks historical documentation — it cannot detect when callers depend on the behavior the ticket wants to change.
**Scope**:
- IN: Modify plugins/dso/agents/intent-search.md to add caller traversal pass, INTENT_CONFLICT signal type with dependency classification (behavioral_dependency vs incidental_usage), convergence protocol (TRAVERSAL_CHECKPOINT after each caller layer, halt at high confidence, cap at 3 levels), remove budget cap enforcement
- OUT: Gate 1a handling of INTENT_CONFLICT in fix-bug SKILL.md (Story 4), user prompt logic

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the intent-search agent performs caller traversal after historical search when classifying as intent-aligned, checking whether callers of affected functions depend on the current behavior
  ← Satisfies: "The enhanced intent-search emits INTENT_CONFLICT with caller evidence and dependency classification"
- When this story is complete, the agent emits INTENT_CONFLICT signal with fields: behavioral_claim, conflicting_callers (file paths + usage snippets), confidence, and dependency_classification (behavioral_dependency or incidental_usage) when callers conflict with the ticket's stated behavioral expectation
  ← Satisfies: "Bug tickets with incorrect behavioral assumptions are caught by the enhanced intent-search"
- When this story is complete, the agent uses a convergence protocol with TRAVERSAL_CHECKPOINT after each caller layer and halts at high confidence, capped at 3 traversal levels, replacing the fixed 20-call budget cap
  ← Satisfies: "The intent-search budget cap is removed; the agent uses depth-first caller traversal with a convergence protocol"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Convergence protocol must handle deep call chains without timeout — 3-level cap is the safety bound; context window is the natural limit beyond that
- [Testing] Test INTENT_CONFLICT emission with synthetic fixtures: ticket claiming "should return empty list" + 3 callers handling None → INTENT_CONFLICT; ticket with no conflicting callers → no INTENT_CONFLICT
- [Reliability] Agent must restrict INTENT_CONFLICT to explicit contradictions between ticket claims and caller invariants — not infer what the fix would do from ticket text

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Done definition update (adversarial review): Budget removal must specify replacement bound. Convergence protocol (3-level cap) replaces budget for caller traversal; historical search steps (3-6) retain the existing budget as advisory. intent_search_budget dispatch parameter retained as advisory, convergence protocol is the primary bound for the new caller traversal pass.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Done definition update (adversarial review): INTENT_CONFLICT output schema must be defined relative to gate-signal-schema.md. INTENT_CONFLICT maps to: gate_id=1a, triggered=true, signal_type=primary, confidence=high/medium, plus additional fields: behavioral_claim, conflicting_callers[], dependency_classification. Story 30c0-6d16 parses these fields.
comments.author: Test
